title: pithecellobium berteroanum
intro: Pithecellobium berteroanum, also known as the "Guam tree" or "Guam ironwood," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a legume native to Guam and the Mariana Islands. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support overall health and well-being.
